source-derived DEM and aerial preview rasters
local_unreal_content_inventory.impact: current captures can improve as source-aware blockouts, but lifelike screenshots require imported production terrain, rock, foliage, water, foam, mist, and lighting assets or first-party/procedural equivalents

official_source_checks.name: Poly Haven
official_source_checks.current_use: candidate CC0 source for HDRIs, textures, and models after asset-level URL and license snapshot records are added

official_source_checks.name: Fab Standard License
official_source_checks.current_use: candidate source for free Unreal-ready environment assets after per-asset price, license tier, collaborator-sharing, and standalone-redistribution checks are recorded

official_source_checks.name: OpenTopography
official_source_checks.current_use: candidate high-resolution topography and hydrology-processing source; API-key and dataset-specific citation/terms review remain required before production terrain import

official_source_checks.name: USGS The National Map
official_source_checks.current_use: official U.S. DEM, hydrography, and imagery source already used for sample pulls; full-corridor source pulls and conditioning remain required

first_party_procedural_equivalent_decisi.decision: Use first-party procedural environment asset recipes as the active replacement path when free/open or licensed photoreal packs are unavailable or not yet cleared.
covered_targets: shore vegetation, deadfall, tropical canopy, and understory
first_party_procedural_equivalent_decisi.promotion_gate: Recipes and concrete material targets are approved as a traceable production path, but a river remains preview-only until they are implemented in Unreal-quality materials/assets, screenshots are captured, guide review passes, and desktop/VR performance evidence is attached.
